check.command: awk -F: 'NR==FNR{uid[$1]=$3; shell[$1]=$7; next} ($1 in uid) && uid[$1]>=1000 && shell[$1] !~ /(nologin|false)$/ && $4 < 1 {print $1 " " $4}' /etc/passwd /etc/shadow

candidate_check.description: RHEL 9 passwords must have a 24 hours minimum password lifetime restriction in /etc/shadow.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify that RHEL 9 has configured the minimum time period between password changes for each user account as one day or greater with the following command:

$ sudo awk -F: '$4 < 1 {printf "%s %d\n", $1, $4}' /etc/shadow

If any results are returned that are not associated with a system account, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Configure noncompliant accounts to enforce a 24 hour minimum password lifetime:

$ sudo passwd -n 1 [user]
title: RHEL 9 passwords must have a 24 hours minimum password lifetime restriction in /etc/shadow.
